primary_source: Whittaker, 1903. On the partial differential equations of mathematical physics. Math. Annalen 57:333-355. DOI: 10.1007/BF01444290.
title: QA Whittaker Rational Direction S^1 PASS [266] - W3 Lipschitz witness g(theta)=sin(theta) at m=9
_fixture_purpose: W3 Lipschitz nearest-neighbor sampling witness at m=9. Test function g(theta) = sin(theta), Lipschitz constant L = 1 on [0, pi/2]. Validator must verify sup_{theta in [0, pi/2]} |g(theta) - g(NN(theta))| <= L * Delta_max^+(9). Empirically (validator computes): actual sup ~ 0.0522 rad; theorem bound L * Delta_max^+(9) ~ 0.1993 rad; ratio ~ 0.26. The fixture also declares a tighter claimed_sup_error_bound = 0.06 (still strictly above actual 0.0522), which validator must verify holds. Both checks pass; fixture is valid.
source_attribution: (Whittaker, 1903) On the partial differential equations of mathematical physics, Math. Annalen 57:333-355, DOI: 10.1007/BF01444290.

lipschitz_witness._note: Theorem bound L*Delta_max^+(9) ~ 0.1993; tighter empirical bound 0.06 also covers actual sup ~ 0.0522.

witnesses.name: lipschitz_function_sin
witnesses.rationale: g(theta) = sin(theta) is 1-Lipschitz in the angular metric on [0, pi/2] since |sin(theta_1) - sin(theta_2)| <= |theta_1 - theta_2| (mean value theorem with |cos| <= 1).
